reasoning: The Lean formalization captures exactly the content of the informal statement. The hypothesis `∀ n, a n ≥ 0` matches "$a_n \geq 0$", and `∃ y, Tendsto (fun n => ∑ i ∈ range n, a i) atTop (𝓝 y)` is the standard Mathlib encoding for convergence of $\sum a_n$. The conclusion `∃ y, Tendsto (fun n => ∑ i ∈ range n, sqrt (a i) / (i+1)) atTop (𝓝 y)` encodes convergence of $\sum \sqrt{a_n}/n$ using the standard 0-indexed trick (`i+1` as denominator to avoid division by zero, matching $n \geq 1$ in the informal). As a universally-quantified statement over all nonneg sequences, this is logically equivalent to the informal version via reindexing. No extra or missing hypotheses; types are correct (ℕ → ℝ, Real.sqrt with open Real).
